item.content:encoded: oh no

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that's a good punchline. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus Christ, that's Jesus Christ! +1
This is my second article to reach +100! Thank you guys!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually laughed aloud at the last one, and I don't mind the whole [redacted] being left up to your own imagination

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! And please do send me a PM if you ever publish that J :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall, I enjoyed this. Interesting concept, good blend of weird and heartwarming, and that expungement at the end is, as others have said, very effective.

Additionally, I now want to make a -J of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
These are actually not normal tables but lookalikes made by stormbreath.

This is ingenious. I've always had trouble trying to get wikidot to format tables the way I want them to look… smart to just find a workaround for it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed! Thank you for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh yeah! Thats definitly not worded right, thanks for noticing.

Glad you enjiyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Small nitpick: in the description there are two lines describing the timeline for the anomaly:

"Two months after acquisition by an individual, SCP-4354 instances develop the abilities to change shape and move through skin cells."

"Four weeks after a subject has been tattooed with an instance of SCP-4354, they will undergo a Superbia Event."

This suggests to me that the subject is tattooed, after a month it pops off their body, and then after *another* month, it starts moving. Which is clearly not the intent. Rephrasing one or both of these sentences to clarify would be ideal.

Otherwise, the Jesus expungement was enough of an "oh shit" to get a +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Going to leave this at a neutral vote for the time being because I feel like the wording could use some tightening up (in particular the phrase "one year straight" strikes me as really un-Foundation for some reason). Otherwise, it is solid and I like the concept. The last entry in the table being expunged other than the picture is a great example of how to use that particular trope well.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Hehe what a beautifully confusing way to phrase that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta love some good old HTML.

The Jesus tattoo got me. Eyebrows went up and thumbs came down.1

Footnotes
1. Onto the upvote button, that is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh my!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for all the nitpicks aismallard! I did my best to work all of them in.

#2 I did not do as I found no way to do it well without dropping the mention of the superbia event, which I did not want to do.
As for the last one: Since they obviously do not want anyone to know what they did here, they also got rid of the designation of the subject, which would probably be linked to a file containing personal data, which could be used to track the subject down and possibly learn about it that way. It is simply a matter of covering up their tracks.

I am glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, Zyn! I am glad you liked it anyway and noticed the tables :D These are actually not normal tables but lookalikes made by stormbreath. The normal wikidot tables don't let the images all be the same size for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! That is what I was hoping to achieve with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice work Crashington! Unique anomaly with a good build-up and payoff at the end.

+ Show nitpicks
- Hide nitpicks

Foundation web crawlers are to search for any mention of SCP-4354 instances. Web content discussing SCP-4354 or SCP-4354-A are to be taken down.

A little redundant, this could just be one sentence about there's a bot that suppresses online information about the anomaly.

All persons affected by SCP-4354 instances are to be sought out and apprehended under the pretence of going through immediate tattoo removal therapy due to a production mistake in the ink used by SCP-4354-A. Apprehended individuals are to be contained in a Standard Humanoid Containment Cell until after the conclusion of the Superbia-Event, at which point they are to be amnesticized and released.

This likewise is a bit wordy, and the two sentences are a bit too similar length-wise. Possibly could be collapsed into one sentence.

SCP-4354 instances develop the abilities to change shape and move through skin cells, as well as to react to outside stimuli. The specific way these abilities manifest is unique for all instances and dependant on the subject of the tattoo.

I feel like this could be described more tersely: you could either describe that they are capable of reacting (since you can't really "react" to something if you can't also change) or dropping the second sentence, since this is elaborated in the actual addendum.

2018/07/21

Nice 👍

Addendum SCP-4354.1: Overview of SCP-4354 instances currently in containment

Would suggest changing it so only "Addendum SCP-4354.1:" is in bold.

As he was being declared deceased shortly after, he regained full consciousness and normal heart activity.

I think the "as he was" part is mildly confusing, maybe swap out with "during the medical examination" or something.

reaching the age of their death within one month. They remained in a coma-like state during this time. When the endpoint was reached, they gained full consciousness

If they're in a "coma-like state" and later become "full[y] conscious", I wouldn't call them dead.

[DATA EXPUNGED]

Lastly, I don't really see a reason why the Jesus one doesn't have a regular designation. If anything they could just blackbox the number?

Anyways these are minor things, good work overall!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nicely done! I particularly like how neat and orderly the tables are. Image size and text distribution are super consistent and visually pleasing.

Normally I'm not a huge fan of body horror, but the tattoos were a great immersion factor. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1ed for Jesus. The rest of the article was very interesting too, and the test log of the two-daughters tattoo was kinda sweet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh oh!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ending is one of the best uses of redaction I've seen recently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can be a factor, I won’t lie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thanks martini :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good skip Crashy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh dang, jesus tattoo. +1

item.title: Re: Novote
item.content:encoded: Thats how it be sometimes :P

item.title: Re: Novote
item.content:encoded: I definitely see where you are coming from now, I just don't feel like it is that big of a deal for the Foundation. Well, I guess we have different headcanons for that kind of things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this how to get you to upvote? :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SNEK

item.title: Re: Novote
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the read!

My intent
– hide block

So, from how i intended this, this isnt about him being a reality bender, or potentially dangerous, but him being literally jedud, with all the memories and implications. This is the son of god and they had to… do something with him right? A big part of foundation employees are probably Christian. How do you explain this to them? They canr just release him to the public, they canr just put him in a normal containment chamber. How would you explain that? So no matter what they did, they had to ensure that people dont know what it was.

Thats how I intended this to be interpreted. Cant guarantee what you take from it of course, but maybe this cleared it up a bit at least.

item.title: Novote
item.content:encoded:
I enjoy the idea behind the article and it is quite obvious what the author is trying to achieve with expungements. However, that section is a bit off. I suppose it just doesn't work for me:

Why the expungements don't exactly do the trick for me
– hide block
  • I initially missed the implication of the SCP-4354-10 tattoo that the tattoos are creating the symbols that they represent, not the image itself. So initially I was like - ok, a Jesus-looking guy is born, not a big deal.
  • After realizing this implication, I am still not exactly worried. As far as I understand, Jesus is not an established character in the Foundation universe, so what we have is a reality bender and not a very powerful one at that. He can transmute and multiply matter, go through walls and levitate, but it is not like he can destroy the world in the matter of seconds or something. And even if he could, the symbols part comes in and Jesus is generally a benevolent character (as far as I know). So the worst that I could imagine is the Foundation having to kill Jesus, but that's not a big deal.
  • And if the Jesus is malevolent - the Foundation kinda knows how to contain reality benders and religious figures, so I am not sure why it is a big problem.

Overall, of course, it is a neat idea and execution, I am just not that enamoured with the expungements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you! And yeah, this was basically the challenge I gave myself with this one.
I'm glad it worked out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Normally, I kinda frown up at the sight of a big meaty slab of [DATA EXPUNGED] in these pieces. I wanted to frown up at this one, but I have to concede, this expungement and its implications did tip the scale on my 'oh-shit'-ometer. The image helped a lot.

+1. Nice. :)

item.title: Author ppppppppppppppppppppppppppppppost
item.content:encoded:
More from this author: http://www.scp-wiki.net/poi-3181-198-file

Hurray! My second solo article!
The idea for this one is almost as old as the first one. Only a few days younger.

This one is mostly about some body-horror and seeing if I can use expungements effectively, as they, in theory, always tickled me. The fact that this is expunged, not redacted, is very deliberate. I tried to make it clear what happens in the superbia event, but that's not the interesting part anyway. The interesting part is the "Further handlings" section. Because what do you do now?

As for -A… treat is as worldbuilding, no need to pay them any attention yet.

Image and critique attribution is in the infobox.

Special thanks to stormbreath for making these awesome and actually functioning tables.
